name: I just got hit by a forklift at my warehouse job—what are the very first things I need to do?
description: Report the injury to your supervisor immediately and seek medical evaluation. Document the scene with photos of the forklift and environment, and gather witness

mainEntity.name: I just got hit by a forklift at my warehouse job—what are the very first things I need to do?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report the injury to your supervisor immediately and seek medical evaluation. Document the scene with photos of the forklift and environment, and gather witness names to ensure your workers\' comp claim has the necessary evidence from day one.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Immediately report the accident to your supervisor and seek medical attention, even if you feel okay. In Utah and Idaho, failing to report the injury in writing quickly can jeopardize your right to benefits. Document the scene by taking photos of the forklift, any floor markings, and the surrounding area before it is cleaned or moved. Collect contact information from any coworkers who witnessed the impact or the mechanical failure. The first 48 hours are critical for establishing that the injury occurred specifically within the scope of your employment.

Prompt reporting creates a paper trail that insurance companies find difficult to dispute later. You must also ensure the medical provider knows this is a work-related incident so the billing is handled correctly through workers\' compensation.

Key factors include:
- The specific time and date of the report to management.
- Availability of witnesses or black-box data from the forklift.
- Medical documentation linking the trauma to the warehouse incident.

Take photos of the forklift\'s serial number and any visible defects. Do not sign any statements from the company\'s insurance adjuster until you understand your rights.

Having an advocate ensures that the employer doesn\'t "lose" evidence or pressure you into a recorded statement that hurts your claim.
